article.description: — Donovan Mitchell scored 40 points, including 14 in the fourth quarter, and the Cleveland Cavaliers overcame an uneven performance for their 15th victory in 16 games, a 114-106 win over the Washington Wizards on Wednesday night.
article.headline: Mitchell scores 40 points as Cavaliers beat Wizards 114-106 for seventh straight win

images.caption: Jordan Poole's 3-point attempt is off the mark and Wizards fans express their displeasure.
images.name: Wizards fans not pleased with Jordan Poole's brick in scoreless night

article.linkText: Mitchell scores 40 points as Cavaliers beat Wizards 114-106 for seventh straight win
article.story: WASHINGTON -- — Donovan Mitchell scored 40 points, including 14 in the fourth quarter, and the Cleveland Cavaliers overcame an uneven performance for their 15th victory in 16 games, a 114-106 win over the Washington Wizards on Wednesday night. Evan Mobley added 22 points on 9-of-10 shooting to help the Cavaliers extend their current NBA-best win streak to seven games and move a game ahead of Milwaukee and New York in second place in the Eastern Conference. “I keep saying it, it's February 7th,” Mitchell said. “This is great, we're in a great position. But at the end of the day, we're trying to play until April, be in the best position (then). Definitely not satisfied with where we're at.” Mitchell and Mobley also had eight rebounds apiece as Cleveland survived a game with nine ties and 17 lead changes against the Wizards, who are second-to-last in the East. The Cavs lost the rebounding battle 47-46 to Washington, the league's worst team on the glass, but held the hosts to just 45% shooting (42 of 94). And they limited Jordan Poole — the Wizards' second-leading scorer — scoreless on five shots before he fouled out in 24-plus minutes. “I think we just do our best to try and make it as difficult as we can on him,” said Cleveland coach J.B. Bickerstaff, whose team has held Poole to 3 of 20 from the floor in three games. “We throw different lineups at him, different matchups. Our big guys do a great job of protecting, but guys have taken an individual challenge on him.” Neither team led by more than seven until Darius Garland's runner was counted good via basket interference to make it 108-99 with 56 seconds remaining. Kyle Kuzma scored 28 points for Washington, which closed a four-game homestand without a victory after winning two of its first three games under interim coach Brian Keefe. Corey Kispert added 23 points and Daniel Gafford had 14 points and 13 rebounds for the Wizards, who hit 16 of their 40 3-point attempts to keep the contest close. “I thought it was more on us,” Mobley said of the Wizards playing Cleveland about even for three quarters. “We just had a lot of mental lapses, started out pretty slow, just weren't knocking down shots that we normally knock down.” The first and second quarters ended even, and Cleveland led 80-79 at the end of the third after Mitchell’s mid-range runner beat the buzzer. Then he scored five points during a 9-0 run in the fourth that gave the Cavaliers the lead for good. And Mitchell's fifth and final 3-pointer — a pull-up over Kuzma with the shot clock expiring — made it 104-97 with 2:19 left. He added two more layups to complete his 18th 40-point game in 108 games with Cleveland. “For us, we didn't start out well, we didn't play our best basketball,” Mitchell said. “For us to kind of still know that, acknowledge that, and being able to fix it ... it's something to fall back on, understanding that we have a foundation.” UP NEXT Cavaliers: At Brooklyn Thursday night. Wizards: At Boston Friday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/NBA

articles.description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks as the favorites to win the NBA championship.
articles.headline: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Spurs favored vs. Knicks entering Finals

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and the Thunder are back to being odds-on title favorites after taking a 3-2 series lead over the Spurs.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [608x342]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: New York Knicks guard Landry Shamet (L) defends Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (R) during game 3 of the NBA East Conference Finals between the Cleveland Cavaliers and the New York Knicks in Cleveland, Ohio, USA, 23 May 2026.
images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.
images.name: Knicks/Cavs [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) takes a shot as San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) defends during the first quarter during game two of the western conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Paycom Center.
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander how has even odds to win the Finals MVP.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander  [1296x729]

images.alt: VIctor Wembanyama
images.caption: VIctor Wembanyama poured in 41 points and grabbed 24 boards in the Spurs' thrilling Game 1 win.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Williams [608x342] - Copy

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) looks for an outlet against San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) during the first half of an NBA basketball game, Tuesday, Jan. 13, 2026, in Oklahoma City.
images.caption: The Thunder enter the Western Conference Finals as the odds-on favorite to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Detroit Pistons guard Cade Cunningham (2) shoots over Cleveland Cavaliers guard Sam Merrill (5) in the second half during game five of the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Little Caesars Arena.
images.caption: Cade Cunningham and the Pistons are favored heading into Game 7.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson #11 of the New York Knicks celebrates during the game against the Philadelphia 76ers during Round Two Game Four of the 2026 NBA Playoffs on May 10, 2026 at the Wells Fargo Center in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images

images.caption: Anthony Edwards returned to the court and helped lead Minnesota to the improbable Game 1 victory.
images.name: Anthony Edwards [1296x729]

images.caption: Cade Cunningham and the Pistons have raced out to a 2-0 series advantage over the Cavs.
images.name: Cade Cunningham [1296x729]

images.caption: The Knicks have surged to +850 to win the NBA Finals following the first-round exit by the Boston Celtics.
